It is located 45 miles (72 km) south of [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") and was designated as a wilderness area in 2002.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Iretaba Peaks Wilderness is managed by the [National Park Service](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service") and the [Bureau of Land Management](/wiki/Bureau_of_Land_Management "Bureau of Land Management").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The western (BLM) portion of the wilderness is part of [Avi Kwa Ame National Monument](/wiki/Avi_Kwa_Ame_National_Monument "Avi Kwa Ame National Monument"), designated in 2023.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Wilderness area in Nevada

| Coordinates | [35°35′39.96″N 114°49′43.92″W﻿ / ﻿35.5944333°N 114.8288667°W﻿ / 35.5944333; -114.8288667](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Ireteba_Peaks_Wilderness&params=35_35_39.96_N_114_49_43.92_W_region:US-NV_type:landmark_dim:12km) |

**Ireteba Peaks Wilderness** is a 32,745 acre (13,251 ha) [wilderness area](/wiki/Wilderness_area "Wilderness area") spanning the area of the southern part of [Eldorado Mountains](/wiki/Eldorado_Mountains "Eldorado Mountains") to the northern parts of [Lake Mohave](/wiki/Lake_Mohave "Lake Mohave"). It is located 45 miles (72 km) south of [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") and was designated as a wilderness area in 2002.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) Iretaba Peaks Wilderness is managed by the [National Park Service](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service") and the [Bureau of Land Management](/wiki/Bureau_of_Land_Management "Bureau of Land Management").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The western (BLM) portion of the wilderness is part of [Avi Kwa Ame National Monument](/wiki/Avi_Kwa_Ame_National_Monument "Avi Kwa Ame National Monument"), designated in 2023.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ireteba_Peaks_Wilderness&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

The peaks were named after one of the leaders of the [Mohave tribe](/wiki/Mohave_people "Mohave people"), [Irataba](/wiki/Irataba "Irataba").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Native vegetation in the area includes [creosote](/wiki/Creosote "Creosote"), [yucca](/wiki/Yucca "Yucca"), [desert willow](/wiki/Chilopsis "Chilopsis"), and several [cacti](/wiki/Cactus "Cactus") species.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) [Desert tortoises](/wiki/Desert_tortoise "Desert tortoise"), [Townsend’s big-eared bats](/wiki/Townsend%27s_big-eared_bat "Townsend's big-eared bat"), and [bighorn sheep](/wiki/Bighorn_sheep "Bighorn sheep") can also be found in Iretaba Peaks Wilderness.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ireteba_Peaks_Wilderness&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: See also")]
